Package org.eventb.core.seqprover.rewriterTests

Class AbstractFormulaRewriterTests

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.seqprover.rewriterTests.AbstractFormulaRewriterTests
Direct Known Subclasses:
ArithFormulaRewriterTests, PredicateSimplifierTests, TypeRewriterTests
public abstract class AbstractFormulaRewriterTests extends Object
Abstract base class for testing formula rewriters.

Main methods are :

  • #rewriteExpr(String, String, String...)
  • #rewritePred(String, String, String...)
  • #noRewriteExpr(String, String...)
  • #noRewritePred(String, String...)

Internally these methods are implemented using a hierarchy of helper classes in order to eradicate code duplication.

    Description

    Description
    protected void
    noRewriteExpr(String inputImage)
    Ensures that the rewriter does not change the given expression.

    Ensures that the rewriter does not change the given expression.

    Ensures that the rewriter does not change the given predicate.

    noRewritePred(String inputImage, String typenvImage)
    Ensures that the rewriter does not change the given predicate.

    rewriteExpr(String inputImage, String expectedImage)
    Test the rewriter for rewriting from an input expression (represented by its string image) to an expected expression (represented by its string image).

    rewriteExpr(String inputImage, String expectedImage, String typenvImage)
    Test the rewriter for rewriting from an input expression (represented by its string image) to an expected expression (represented by its string image).
    protected void
    rewriteExpr(String inputImage, String expectedImage, String typenvImage, boolean rewrite)
    Test the rewriter for rewriting from an input expression (represented by its string image) to an expected expression (represented by its string image).
    protected void
    rewritePred(String inputImage, String expectedImage)
    Test the rewriter for rewriting from an input predicate (represented by its string image) to an expected predicate (represented by its string image).
    protected void
    rewritePred(String inputImage, String expectedImage, String typenvImage)
    Test the rewriter for rewriting from an input predicate (represented by its string image) to an expected predicate (represented by its string image).
    protected void
    rewritePred(String inputImage, String expectedImage, String typenvImage, boolean rewrite)
    Tests the rewriter for rewriting conditionally from an input predicate (represented by its string image) to an expected predicate (represented by its string image).
    protected void
    rewritePredEmptySet(String inputImage, String expectedImage, String typenvImage, boolean rewrite)
    Tests the rewriter for rewriting conditionally from an empty set input predicate to an expected predicate (represented by its string image).

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractFormulaRewriterTests

      protected AbstractFormulaRewriterTests(FormulaFactory ff, IFormulaRewriter rewriter)
      Client extending this class must provide the rewriter which is tested.
      Parameters:
      rewriter - the rewriter under test

  • Method Details

    • rewritePred

      protected void rewritePred(String inputImage, String expectedImage, String typenvImage)
      Test the rewriter for rewriting from an input predicate (represented by its string image) to an expected predicate (represented by its string image).

      The type environment is described as usual. For instance, to describe a type environment where S is a given set and x is an integer, one would pass the strings "S=ℙ(S); x=ℤ".

      Parameters:
      inputImage - the string image of the input predicate
      expectedImage - the string image of the expected predicate
      typenvImage - the string image of the type environment

    • rewritePred

      protected void rewritePred(String inputImage, String expectedImage, String typenvImage, boolean rewrite)
      Tests the rewriter for rewriting conditionally from an input predicate (represented by its string image) to an expected predicate (represented by its string image).

      The type environment is described as usual. For instance, to describe a type environment where S is a given set and x is an integer, one would pass the strings "S=ℙ(S); x=ℤ".

      Parameters:
      inputImage - the string image of the input predicate
      expectedImage - the string image of the expected predicate
      typenvImage - the string image of the type environment
      rewrite - tells whether the predicate shall be rewritten

    • rewritePred

      protected void rewritePred(String inputImage, String expectedImage)
      Test the rewriter for rewriting from an input predicate (represented by its string image) to an expected predicate (represented by its string image).

      Both predicates must type-check by themselves without a given type environment.

      Parameters:
      inputImage - the string image of the input predicate
      expectedImage - the string image of the expected predicate

    • rewritePredEmptySet

      protected void rewritePredEmptySet(String inputImage, String expectedImage, String typenvImage, boolean rewrite)
      Tests the rewriter for rewriting conditionally from an empty set input predicate to an expected predicate (represented by its string image). The input empty set predicate is composed from a given input expression (represented by its string image).

      The type environment is described as usual. For instance, to describe a type environment where S is a given set and x is an integer, one would pass the strings "S=ℙ(S); x=ℤ".

      Parameters:
      inputImage - the string image of the input predicate
      expectedImage - the string image of the output predicate
      typenvImage - the string image of the type environment
      rewrite - tells whether the predicate shall be rewritten

    • noRewritePred

      protected void noRewritePred(String inputImage, String typenvImage)
      Ensures that the rewriter does not change the given predicate.

      The type environment is described as usual. For instance, to describe a type environment where S is a given set and x is an integer, one would pass the strings "S=ℙ(S); x=ℤ".

      Parameters:
      inputImage - the string image of the input predicate
      typenvImage - the string image of the type environment

    • noRewritePred

      protected void noRewritePred(String inputImage)
      Ensures that the rewriter does not change the given predicate.

      The predicate must type-check by itself without a given type environment.

      Parameters:
      inputImage - the string image of the input predicate

    • rewriteExpr

      protected void rewriteExpr(String inputImage, String expectedImage, String typenvImage)
      Test the rewriter for rewriting from an input expression (represented by its string image) to an expected expression (represented by its string image).

      The type environment is described as usual. For instance, to describe a type environment where S is a given set and x is an integer, one would pass the strings "S=ℙ(S); x=ℤ".

      Parameters:
      inputImage - the string image of the input expression.
      expectedImage - the string image of the expected expression.
      typenvImage - the string image of the type environment

    • rewriteExpr

      protected void rewriteExpr(String inputImage, String expectedImage, String typenvImage, boolean rewrite)
      Test the rewriter for rewriting from an input expression (represented by its string image) to an expected expression (represented by its string image).

      The type environment is described as usual. For instance, to describe a type environment where S is a given set and x is an integer, one would pass the strings "S=ℙ(S); x=ℤ".

      Parameters:
      inputImage - the string image of the input expression.
      expectedImage - the string image of the expected expression.
      typenvImage - the string image of the type environment
      rewrite - tells whether the predicate shall be rewritten

    • rewriteExpr

      protected void rewriteExpr(String inputImage, String expectedImage)
      Test the rewriter for rewriting from an input expression (represented by its string image) to an expected expression (represented by its string image).

      Both expressions must type-check by themselves without a given type environment.

      Parameters:
      inputImage - the string image of the input expression.
      expectedImage - the string image of the expected expression.

    • noRewriteExpr

      protected void noRewriteExpr(String inputImage, String typenvImage)
      Ensures that the rewriter does not change the given expression.

      The type environment is described as usual. For instance, to describe a type environment where S is a given set and x is an integer, one would pass the strings "S=ℙ(S); x=ℤ".

      Parameters:
      inputImage - the string image of the input expression.
      typenvImage - the string image of the type environment

    • noRewriteExpr

      protected void noRewriteExpr(String inputImage)
      Ensures that the rewriter does not change the given expression.

      The expression must type-check by itself without a given type environment.

      Parameters:
      inputImage - the string image of the input expression.
